clio.name: Clio
clio.short_description: Cloud-based legal practice management software for case management, time tracking, billing, and client intake.

features.description: Centralizes documents, contacts, communications, tasks, and billing on a single matter record for each client case.
features.title: Matter & Case Management

features.description: Captures billable time and expenses from desktop or mobile and feeds entries directly into invoices.
features.title: Time & Expense Tracking

features.description: Generates customizable invoices, supports online payments, and automates billing reminders.
features.title: Billing & Invoicing

features.description: Helps firms track client retainer funds and stay compliant with IOLTA and similar trust accounting rules.
features.title: Trust Accounting

features.description: Captures leads through web intake forms, automates follow-up, and reports on referral sources before a matter is opened.
features.title: Client Intake & CRM (Clio Grow)

features.description: Generates court forms and templated documents automatically from matter data.
features.title: Document Automation (Clio Draft)

features.description: Gives clients a secure space to message the firm, view case updates, and pay invoices online.
features.title: Client Portal

features.description: Provides visibility into billable-hour trends, matter profitability, and staff utilization.
features.title: Reporting & Analytics

features.description: Native iOS and Android apps let attorneys track time, capture receipts, and review matters remotely.
features.title: Mobile Apps

features.description: Connects Clio to email, calendaring, accounting, and e-signature tools such as Outlook, Gmail, and QuickBooks.
features.title: App Marketplace & Integrations

pros: All-in-one platform reduces double data entry across intake, billing, and case management
Strong trust accounting support built for US and Canadian compliance requirements
Large marketplace of third-party integrations
Mature, well-established product with extensive onboarding, documentation, and support resources
Native mobile apps for time tracking and case access on the go
cons: No permanent free plan; costs rise quickly once add-ons like Clio Grow are included
Reporting, workflow automation, and client intake features are locked behind higher-priced tiers
Cloud-only, so firms needing on-premises hosting or strict data residency options must look elsewhere
Initial setup and data migration can be time-consuming for firms switching from legacy systems
Per-user pricing can become expensive for larger firms compared to flat-fee alternatives

faqs.answer: No. Clio does not offer a permanent free plan; it offers a time-limited free trial, after which firms must choose a paid plan.
faqs.question: Is Clio free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes. Clio Manage includes trust accounting tools designed to help firms track client retainer funds in line with IOLTA and similar rules.
faqs.question: Does Clio support trust accounting?

faqs.answer: Yes. Clio's entry-level plan is aimed at solo practitioners and small firms that need case management, billing, and time tracking in one system.
faqs.question: Is Clio suitable for solo attorneys?

faqs.answer: Clio Manage handles case management, billing, and trust accounting after a matter is opened, while Clio Grow handles client intake and CRM before and during the intake process.
faqs.question: What is the difference between Clio Manage and Clio Grow?

faqs.answer: Yes. Clio integrates with QuickBooks and other third-party tools through its app marketplace.
faqs.question: Does Clio integrate with accounting software like QuickBooks?

features: Clio Grow client intake and CRM included

bubble.name: Bubble
bubble.short_description: Bubble is a visual no-code platform for building and hosting full web applications with a database, logic, and frontend—no coding required.
bubble.headquarters: New York City, New York, USA

features.description: Drag-and-drop, pixel-level control over layout and responsive design, with reusable elements and style guides.
features.title: Visual page editor

features.description: Define custom data types, fields, and relationships to power app content without a separate backend service.
features.title: Built-in relational database

features.description: Create conditional, event-driven logic (backend and frontend workflows) without writing traditional code.
features.title: Visual workflow engine

features.description: Native support for user sign-up, login, roles, and permissions built into the platform.
features.title: User authentication

features.description: Connect to virtually any external REST API or web service to pull in or send data.
features.title: API Connector

features.description: A large ecosystem of plugins and starter templates for payments, maps, chat, and other common app needs.
features.title: Plugin and template marketplace

features.description: Package and deploy Bubble apps to the iOS App Store and Google Play on eligible paid plans.
features.title: App store deployment

features.description: Manage development and live branches so changes can be tested safely before affecting a live application.
features.title: Version control

features.description: Launch apps on your own domain with hosting managed by Bubble on paid plans.
features.title: Custom domains and hosting

pros: Capable of building full, complex applications (database + logic + frontend), not just simple sites
Large plugin and template marketplace speeds up development
No coding required, yet offers deep customization compared to simpler no-code tools
Active community forum and educational resources for learning the platform
Handles hosting and deployment, including app store packaging on paid plans
cons: Steep learning curve compared to simpler website or app builders
Workload Unit-based pricing can become expensive as an app scales
Apps are hosted on Bubble's infrastructure, limiting portability and control
Performance can require active tuning for database-heavy or complex apps
Free plan is limited and does not support custom domains or production deployment

faqs.answer: Yes. Bubble lets users build the frontend, database, and backend logic of a web application entirely through a visual editor, without writing traditional code, though building complex apps still requires learning Bubble's own logic model.
faqs.question: Is Bubble really no-code?

faqs.answer: Bubble apps are built as responsive web apps by default, but paid plans allow you to package and deploy them to the iOS App Store and Google Play as native-feeling mobile apps.
faqs.question: Can I build a mobile app with Bubble?

faqs.answer: Yes, Bubble offers a free plan for learning and prototyping, though it comes with usage limits and does not support custom domains or production-level deployment.
faqs.question: Does Bubble have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Paid plans are priced around a monthly allotment of Workload Units, which measure the server resources your app consumes (database queries, workflows, API calls, page loads); higher tiers include more Workload Units and additional features like advanced version control.
faqs.question: How does Bubble's pricing work?

faqs.answer: Startup founders building MVPs, no-code agencies building client apps, and internal tools teams at larger companies are among the most common Bubble users.
faqs.question: Who typically uses Bubble?

features: No custom domain or production deployment
Deploy to the web or app stores
Support for two app editors and ten custom branches
Designed for collaboration and higher app usage

trustarc.name: TrustArc
trustarc.short_description: Enterprise privacy management platform for consent, data mapping, DSAR automation, assessments, and regulatory compliance.

features.description: Scans websites for cookies and trackers, then generates configurable consent banners and preference centers to support GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and similar consent laws.
features.title: Cookie Consent Manager

features.description: Helps teams discover, map, and maintain a living inventory of where personal data resides and how it flows across systems and vendors.
features.title: Data Inventory Hub

features.description: Automates the workflow for running privacy and data protection impact assessments across business units, replacing manual spreadsheet-based processes.
features.title: Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) Manager

features.description: Applies structured assessment workflows to third parties and vendors to evaluate and track their privacy and data handling risk.
features.title: Vendor Risk Management

features.description: Manages intake, routing, fulfillment, and reporting for data subject access requests, helping teams meet statutory response deadlines.
features.title: Privacy Rights Automation (DSAR)

features.description: An AI-powered assistant that provides cited, context-aware answers to privacy questions inside the platform, aimed at reducing reliance on outside counsel.
features.title: Ask Arc AI Assistant

features.description: Checks an organization's data processing activities against a large library of rules drawn from privacy laws in jurisdictions around the world.
features.title: Risk Profile & Regulatory Intelligence

features.description: Third-party privacy certification and verification services, descended from TrustArc's original TRUSTe seal program, used as an external trust signal.
features.title: Assurance & Certification Services

features.description: Centralized reporting to demonstrate compliance status, track response-time obligations, and provide audit trails across privacy workflows.
features.title: Reporting & Compliance Dashboards

pros: Centralizes and automates privacy impact assessments and data mapping that would otherwise be managed manually
Long operating history and deep regulatory content library built up since the company's founding as TRUSTe
Praised by reviewers for an approachable, user-friendly interface once the platform is configured
Broad module coverage spanning consent, data mapping, DSARs, vendor risk, and certification in one vendor
Responsive support team, according to a number of recent user reviews
cons: Enterprise pricing model, with reported annual contracts commonly reaching into the tens of thousands of dollars, is out of reach for small businesses
No public API, which limits programmatic integration and embedding of consent workflows into custom applications
Initial implementation and configuration are described by multiple reviewers as technically demanding
Some data-mapping and linking features are noted by users as needing improvement
Support can be inconsistent, with some reviews describing a reactive rather than proactive approach to harder issues

faqs.answer: TrustArc is a privacy management platform that helps organizations handle cookie consent, data inventory and mapping, privacy impact assessments, vendor risk management, and data subject access requests in order to comply with laws like GDPR and CCPA/CPRA.
faqs.question: What does TrustArc do?

faqs.answer: TrustArc does not publish standard pricing; it sells modules through custom enterprise contracts scoped to an organization's needs, and third-party marketplace data suggests typical annual contracts can range from roughly ten thousand to well over a hundred thousand dollars depending on modules and volume.
faqs.question: How much does TrustArc cost?

faqs.answer: TrustArc is sold through a sales-led enterprise process rather than self-service signup, and there is no confirmed public self-serve free trial; prospective customers typically need to contact TrustArc directly for a demo and quote.
faqs.question: Does TrustArc offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Yes. TrustArc began in 1997 as TRUSTe, an online privacy certification organization, and rebranded to TrustArc in 2017 as it expanded into full privacy management software.
faqs.question: Is TrustArc the same company as TRUSTe?

faqs.answer: According to user reviews, TrustArc does not currently offer a public API, which can limit how deeply engineering teams can integrate its consent and privacy workflows into their own applications.
faqs.question: Does TrustArc have a public API for developers?

features: Cookie Consent Manager and website tracking scans
Consent banners and preference center configuration
Data Inventory Hub for data discovery and mapping
PIA Manager for automated privacy impact assessments
Privacy Rights Automation for DSAR handling

features.description: GraphQL-native architecture that combines multiple data sources without duplication.

SOC 2 Type 2 (since 2022) and ISO 27001 certified.
MCP Server API allows AI assistants to connect to content.
GDPR and German data-protection (BDSG/TMG) compliant.

SSO, multitenancy, and dedicated infrastructure require custom Enterprise pricing.
Hobby plan is capped at 1,000 entries and 500,000 API calls per month.
Founding year and headquarters are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.answer: Yes, the Hobby plan is free forever, with 3 seats and unlimited asset storage up to certain usage limits.
faqs.question: Is Hygraph free?

faqs.answer: Yes, its GraphQL-native architecture can combine multiple data sources without duplicating data.
faqs.question: Does Hygraph support content federation?

faqs.answer: Yes, it has been SOC 2 Type 2 certified since August 2022, and is also ISO 27001 certified.
faqs.question: Is Hygraph SOC 2 certified?

faqs.answer: Yes, via the MCP Server API designed for AI assistant integration.

listmonk.name: Listmonk
listmonk.short_description: Listmonk is a free, open-source, self-hosted newsletter and mailing list manager delivered as a single Go binary with a Postgres backend.

features.description: Ships as a single Go binary (or Docker image) with no external runtime dependencies beyond PostgreSQL.
features.title: Single-binary deployment

features.description: Multi-threaded SMTP queues with rate limiting designed to handle mailing lists with millions of subscribers.
features.title: High-throughput sending

features.description: Built-in tracking for opens, clicks, bounces, and link performance.
features.title: Campaign analytics

features.description: Drag-and-drop visual builder alongside Go-template-based dynamic email templating.

features.description: API for sending one-off transactional messages in addition to bulk campaigns.

features.description: HTTP webhook-based messenger interfaces for extending delivery to channels beyond email.
features.title: Extensible messengers

features.description: Single sign-on support for admin authentication.
features.title: OIDC SSO

pros: Completely free and open source (AGPLv3) with no paid tier
Single binary is simple to deploy and self-host
Designed to scale to millions of subscribers with multi-threaded sending queues
Built-in analytics reduce the need for separate tracking tools
cons: Requires self-hosting and a PostgreSQL database; no official managed cloud version from the maintainers
No built-in SMTP sending service — you must supply your own SMTP/mail provider
AGPLv3 license may impose obligations for organizations building derivative hosted services

nocodb.name: NocoDB
nocodb.short_description: NocoDB is a no-code database platform that turns spreadsheets or existing SQL databases into collaborative, self-hostable apps.

features.description: Grid, Gallery, Form, Kanban, and Calendar views over the same underlying data.
features.title: Multiple views

features.description: Linked records, lookups, rollups, and formula fields with create/read/update/delete operations.
features.title: Spreadsheet-style editing

features.description: Point NocoDB at an existing Postgres or MySQL database instead of migrating data into its own storage.
features.title: Connect to existing databases

features.description: Every base gets REST APIs, webhooks, and an SDK for programmatic access and automation.
features.title: Auto-generated REST APIs

features.description: Fine-grained permissions across five user roles.
features.title: Role-based access control

features.description: Built-in connectors for Slack, Discord, AWS SES/S3, SMTP, and other services.
features.title: App Store integrations

features.description: Free deployment via Docker or binary installers for macOS, Linux, and Windows.
features.title: Self-hosted Community Edition

features.description: Single- and multi-provider AI options available on Business and Scale plans.
features.title: AI on paid tiers

pros: Free, unlimited-seat self-hosted Community Edition
Can connect directly to an existing Postgres or MySQL database instead of requiring data migration
Large community per vendor claims: 64k+ GitHub stars and 20M+ Docker downloads
Auto-generated REST APIs and a built-in App Store of integrations
Both cloud-hosted and self-hosted deployment options, with a genuine free cloud tier
cons: The self-hosted Community Edition moved from AGPLv3 to the Sustainable Use License, a source-available license rather than an OSI-approved open-source license, which drew criticism from parts of the self-hosting community
Cloud free plan is capped at 1,000 records and 1GB storage
SSO, audit logs, and row-level security are reserved for Business/Scale/Enterprise tiers
The Scale plan requires a minimum of 3 seats

faqs.answer: Yes. NocoDB offers a free cloud plan (3 editor seats, 1,000 records) and a free, self-hosted Community Edition.
faqs.question: Is NocoDB free?

faqs.answer: The self-hosted Community Edition is distributed under the Sustainable Use License, a source-available license, after the project moved away from AGPLv3.
faqs.question: What license does NocoDB use?

faqs.answer: Yes, NocoDB can turn an existing Postgres or MySQL database into a spreadsheet-style interface rather than requiring data import.
faqs.question: Can NocoDB connect to an existing database?

faqs.answer: Yes, every base gets auto-generated REST APIs, webhooks, and an SDK.
faqs.question: Does NocoDB provide an API?

faqs.answer: Yes, via Docker, binary installers for macOS/Linux/Windows, or an install script.
faqs.question: Is NocoDB available self-hosted?

keystonejs.name: KeystoneJS
keystonejs.short_description: KeystoneJS is a free, open-source Node.js headless CMS that generates a GraphQL API and admin UI from a schema you define.

features.description: Define your data schema once and Keystone automatically generates a full GraphQL API with pagination, sorting, filtering, and CRUD operations.
features.title: Schema-driven GraphQL API

features.description: A customizable admin interface is generated from your schema, including a rich text WYSIWYG editor for content editing.
features.title: Auto-generated Admin UI

features.description: Built-in access control lets developers restrict operations at the field, list, and operation level.
features.title: Fine-grained access control

features.description: Database schema migrations are handled automatically through Prisma.
features.title: Prisma-based migrations

features.description: Includes built-in user session handling for authentication flows.
features.title: Session management

features.description: Supports event hooks and custom GraphQL queries/mutations for extending default behavior.
features.title: Custom logic via hooks

features.description: Written in TypeScript (99% of the codebase) with full TypeScript or JavaScript support for application code.
features.title: TypeScript-first

pros: Free and fully open source under the MIT license, with no vendor lock-in
Auto-generates both a GraphQL API and an admin UI from a single schema definition
Over 1 million npm installs and 250+ contributors, per the project's own site
Deployable to a wide range of hosts (DigitalOcean, Heroku, Render, Vercel, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) or custom infrastructure
Database migrations handled automatically via Prisma
cons: Requires Node.js development skills — it is a developer framework, not a no-code tool
Keystone 5 is in maintenance mode; only Keystone 6 receives active development
Paid support is only available as a separate enterprise contract through Thinkmill, not a packaged pricing tier
No official managed hosting/cloud product — self-hosting or third-party PaaS deployment is required

faqs.answer: Yes, KeystoneJS is free and open source under the MIT license. Paid enterprise support is available separately through Thinkmill.
faqs.question: Is KeystoneJS free?

faqs.answer: Defining a schema in Keystone automatically produces a GraphQL API with CRUD, pagination, sorting, and filtering, plus a customizable admin UI.
faqs.question: What does KeystoneJS generate from my schema?

faqs.answer: Keystone uses Prisma to handle database migrations automatically.
faqs.question: What database tooling does KeystoneJS use?

faqs.answer: Keystone 5 is in maintenance mode; Keystone 6 (published under the @keystone-6/* npm namespace) is the actively developed version.
faqs.question: Is Keystone 5 still supported?

faqs.answer: KeystoneJS is developed and maintained by Thinkmill, an Australian design and development consultancy, with contributions from 250+ people since the project's first commit in 2013.
faqs.question: Who maintains KeystoneJS?

pros: Core edition is free forever with no per-site licensing fee

Headless delivery via REST API and GraphQL (Pro)
Active Discord community and a network of certified partner agencies
cons: Multi-user accounts, revisions/drafts, and API access require the paid Pro license ($349/site + $99/year)
Requires PHP/Laravel hosting and development knowledge, unlike no-code site builders
Core team is small (6 full-time members)
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published

faqs.answer: Statamic Core is free forever. The Pro tier costs $349 per site for the first year (including updates), then $99 per year for continued updates.

faqs.answer: Statamic is built on the Laravel PHP framework.

faqs.answer: Yes, REST API and GraphQL access are available with a Statamic Pro license.
faqs.question: Does Statamic support headless or API-based use?

faqs.answer: Yes, Statamic can run in flat-file mode, storing content as files rather than in a database.

faqs.answer: Statamic was founded in 2012 by Jack McDade and Mubashar Iqbal.

features.title: Flexible deployment

features.description: Runs natively on Windows 10+, macOS 12+, and 64-bit Linux.
features.title: Cross-platform desktop app

pros: Free and open source under the GPL-3.0 license
Generates static sites, so there's no server-side hosting to maintain or secure
Wide range of built-in deployment targets (GitHub Pages, Netlify, S3, etc.)
Built-in SEO and GDPR/CCPA compliance tooling
Works fully offline as a local desktop app
cons: Desktop-only workflow with no browser-based editing
Publishing requires a manual build-and-deploy step from the app
Premium themes and plugins in the marketplace cost extra
No built-in multi-user/team collaboration

faqs.answer: Yes, the core Publii desktop app is free and open source under the GPL-3.0 license. Optional premium themes and plugins are sold separately in its marketplace.
faqs.question: Is Publii free to use?

faqs.answer: Publii is a desktop application available for Windows 10+, macOS 12+, and 64-bit Linux.
faqs.question: What platforms does Publii run on?

faqs.answer: Publii can deploy to GitHub Pages, GitLab, Netlify, Amazon S3, Google Cloud, SFTP/FTPS, or manually export files.
faqs.question: Where can I host a site built with Publii?

faqs.answer: Yes. Publii is built with Electron and Vue.js and its source code is available on GitHub under the GPL-3.0 license.
faqs.question: Is Publii open source?

features: Deployment to GitHub Pages, Netlify, S3, and more

onedev.name: OneDev
onedev.short_description: OneDev is a self-hosted DevOps platform unifying Git, issues, pull requests, CI/CD, and packages, with a free Community Edition.

features.description: Combines Git, issues, pull requests, CI/CD, packages, and workspaces in one place.
features.title: Unified DevOps platform

features.description: Per-commit code search with language-aware symbol detection.
features.title: Code search and navigation

features.description: CI/CD as code with a GUI editor and multiple executor types, including containers, Kubernetes, agents, and bare metal.
features.title: Built-in CI/CD

features.description: Built-in registries supporting Docker, NPM, Maven, NuGet, PyPI, RubyGems, and Helm.
features.title: Package registries

features.description: Task management with customizable issue workflows.
features.title: Automated Kanban boards

features.description: Email-to-issue linking for support and request handling.
features.title: Service desk

features.description: AI integration described as supporting autonomous development work.
features.title: AI user automation

pros: Free Community Edition includes built-in CI/CD, package registries, code search, SSO/2FA, and LDAP/AD support
Combines Git hosting, issue tracking, CI/CD, and packages in a single self-hosted platform
Enterprise Edition adds HA/scaling, security scanning, and audit logging for $6/user/month
Includes AI user automation for autonomous development work
cons: Enterprise Edition requires a minimum order of 12 user-months
Security/compliance scanning, cross-project search, and HA clustering are Enterprise-only, not in the free Community Edition
Self-hosted only — no managed cloud offering found on the official site

faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free and includes a built-in CI/CD engine, package registries, code search, and SSO/2FA.
faqs.question: Is OneDev free?

faqs.answer: $6 per user/month, with a minimum order of 12 user-months.
faqs.question: What does OneDev Enterprise cost?

faqs.answer: Docker, NPM, Maven, NuGet, PyPI, RubyGems, and Helm.
faqs.question: What package formats does OneDev support?

faqs.answer: Yes, a built-in CI/CD engine with a GUI editor and multiple executor types: containers, Kubernetes, agents, and bare metal.
faqs.question: Does OneDev include CI/CD?

faqs.answer: The site lists AI integration for autonomous development work and AI user automation among its included capabilities.
faqs.question: Does OneDev have AI features?

features: Package registry (Docker, NPM, Maven, NuGet, PyPI, RubyGems, Helm)
